{"query":"Anthony Albanese Australia Prime Minister official remarks Julian Assange arrival release June 26 2024","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1815,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/2/15/enough-is-enough-australian-pm-denounces-us-uk-legal-pursuit-of-assange","title":"‘Enough is enough’: Australian PM denounces US, UK legal pursuit of Assange | Julian Assange News | Al Jazeera","content":"Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has slammed the years-long United States and British legal pursuit of WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange ahead of a court ruling on his appeal against extradition next week.
He has spent five years in London’s high-security Belmarsh Prison, battling extradition to the US, where he is wanted on criminal charges over the release of confidential military records and diplomatic cables in 2010.
Assange was arrested after spending seven years in Ecuador’s embassy in London to avoid extradition to Sweden, where he faced accusations of sexual assault, which were later dropped.
Reporting from London, Al Jazeera’s Sonia Gallego said that his supporters fear that Assange could essentially be “put behind bars for the rest of his life”."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/media/video/2024/jun/26/julian-assange-wikileaks-anthony-albanese-australia","title":"Assange 'generous in praise' of Australia's effort to secure his release, says Albanese – video | Julian Assange | The Guardian","content":"This article is more than 2 years old
The Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, said he spoke with the recently freed WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange after he arrived in his home country, Australia.
Albanese said: 'I had a very warm discussion with him this evening."},{"url":"https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2024/06/26/julian-assange-wikileaks-australia/","title":"Julian Assange arrives home in Australia amid warnings about press freedom - The Washington Post","content":"CANBERRA, Australia — Julian Assange stepped out of his chartered airplane and paused for a moment, taking a breath of chilly Canberra evening air.
Then the Australian founder of WikiLeaks raised his fist triumphantly and stepped onto home soil for the first time in more than a decade, waving to his supporters as he crossed the tarmac before at last embracing his wife as a free man."},{"url":"https://www.pm.gov.au/media","title":"Media | Prime Minister of Australia","content":"Stay up to date with the Prime Minister.
This section lists media releases, statements, transcripts, speeches and other media items including joint releases.
Over 800,000 free TAFE enrolments delivering the skills Australians want, for the jobs we need"},{"url":"https://theconversation.com/julian-assange-told-anthony-albanese-he-saved-his-life-after-landing-in-australia-233348","title":"Julian Assange told Anthony Albanese he ‘saved his life’ after landing in Australia","content":"Julian Assange told Anthony Albanese he had “saved his life” in his first phone conversation after his chartered plane’s wheels touched down at Canberra’s RAAF base soon after 7:30pm on Wednesday.
The prime minister, who has never met Assange, told a news conference at Parliament House immediately afterwards that Assange had described his Australian arrival as “a surreal and happy moment”.
Albanese said the conversation was “very warm” and that Assange was “very generous in his praise of the Australian government”.
Smith, who had accompanied him from the UK, and Rudd were both with him on the last leg of his journey to Canberra.
Stella Assange also talked about how vulnerable the press had become since Assange was indicted under the US Espionage Act."}]}
